name: plans-handler
description: Manage subscription plans, pricing, and quotas. Use when adding plan features, updating limits, or building pricing pages.
capabilities: Add new quota limits to database schema, validation, and UI forms
Create pricing tables that fetch plans via API and display features
Access user subscription plans on client and server side
Maintain consistency between database, validation, and form schemas
Generate subscription URLs for different payment providers
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: All 29 static findings are false positives. The backtick patterns are markdown code fences, not shell execution. Network and filesystem references are JSON metadata fields. No actual security risks detected in skill code.
valueStatement: Managing subscription plans and quotas across database, validation, and UI layers is error-prone and time-consuming. This skill provides a structured workflow for adding plan features, updating limits, and building consistent pricing pages.
limitations: Does not create or modify payment provider accounts (Stripe, PayPal)
Does not handle actual billing transactions or webhooks
Does not migrate existing plans between providers
Requires user to configure plan settings via dashboard UI

useCases.title: Add New Quota to Subscription Plans
useCases.description: Add a new usage-based limit like 'max_projects' or 'storage_gb' across database schema, form validation, and admin UI components.

useCases.title: Build Pricing Page
useCases.description: Create a pricing table component that displays available plans, features, and subscription buttons using the plans API.

useCases.title: Check User Subscription Status
useCases.description: Retrieve the current user's plan and quota usage on the client using SWR hooks or on the server with authentication wrappers.

promptTemplates.title: Add New Plan Limit
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add a new quota limit called '{quota_name}' to our subscription plans. Follow the plans-handler workflow: update the database schema, add validation, and create the UI input.
promptTemplates.scenario: When adding a new usage-based limit to subscription plans

promptTemplates.title: Create Pricing Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pricing table component for our subscription plans. It should fetch plans via API, display features from plan.quotas, and include subscribe buttons with proper URLs.
promptTemplates.scenario: When building a pricing page for customers

promptTemplates.title: Get User Plan Info
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to access the current user's subscription plan. I need examples for both client-side (SWR) and server-side (withAuthRequired) implementations.
promptTemplates.target_user: When implementing feature access based on subscription

promptTemplates.title: Sync Plan Schemas
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit our plan-related files and ensure the database schema, validation schema, and form schema are all in sync. Report any mismatches.

bestPractices: Always use the Super Admin dashboard UI for plan changes instead of manual database seeding
Keep database schema, validation schema, and form schema in sync when adding quotas
Use usage-based quota names like 'images' rather than time-bound names like 'monthly_images'
antiPatterns: Manually seeding plans directly in the database instead of using the dashboard
Adding a quota to only one layer (DB, validation, or UI) and forgetting others
Hardcoding plan IDs or provider IDs instead of configuring them in the plan dashboard

faq.question: What quota naming convention should I use?
faq.answer: Use usage-based names like 'images' or 'storage_gb' rather than time-bound names like 'monthly_images'. This makes quotas more flexible for different billing periods.

faq.question: How do I add a new quota limit?
faq.answer: Add the field to three places: quotaSchema in database schema, planFormSchema in validation, and the plan form UI. Then update the plan via the Super Admin dashboard.

faq.question: How do I access a user's plan on the client?
faq.answer: Use the useCurrentPlan() hook with SWR. For server-side access, use the withAuthRequired middleware wrapper and call getCurrentPlan().

faq.question: Can I create plans manually in the database?
faq.answer: No. Always use the Super Admin dashboard (/super-admin/plans) to create or modify plans. Manual seeding can cause inconsistencies.

faq.question: How do I create a subscribe button?
faq.answer: Use the getSubscribeUrl helper function from src/lib/plans/getSubscribeUrl.ts with parameters: { codename, type, provider }.

faq.question: What if my schemas are out of sync?
faq.answer: Audit the three files: src/db/schema/plans.ts, src/lib/validations/plan.schema.ts, and src/components/forms/plan-form.tsx. Ensure all new quotas are added to each.
